steps.title: Join clauses with contrast
steps.content: The first clause says one might assume such misnaming cases are rare. The second clause says the opposite: they are part of a whole list of examples. The conjunctive adverb on the contrary needs a semicolon before it and a comma after it.

steps.title: Avoid comma splices
steps.content: Choice A joins independent clauses with only commas. Choice B puts the semicolon after the transition instead of before it. Choice C gives no punctuation after rare. The answer is D.
